steps.title: Interpret the intercept
steps.content: The $x$-intercept is the point on the graph where $y=0$. In this context, $y$ represents the approximate number of crates that remain to be unloaded. So $y=0$ means there are no crates left on the truck.

steps.title: Find the time
steps.content:
Set $y$ equal to $0$ in the model and solve for $x$. This gives the number of hours after the crew started working when the remaining-crates amount reaches $0$:
$$0=126-28x$$
$$28x=126$$
$$x=\frac{126}{28}=4.5$$

steps.title: Match the context
steps.content: The value $x=4.5$ represents about $4.5$ hours after the crew started unloading. Since the corresponding $y$-value is $0$, the crew will have unloaded all the crates at that time. The answer is A.
